articles.title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
articles.description:
Nearly 11,000 special train tickets were sold in the first hour of availability alone, according to transit officials.

The post Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Commuter Rail ticket sales for the World Cup surpassed 11,000 in the first hour.
Transit officials reported record-breaking demand for special train tickets on April 9.
Increased train usage may affect local transit schedules and capacity for Boston residents.

articles.title: A federal judge dismisses another DOJ lawsuit seeking voter data, this time in Massachusetts
articles.description:
The judge said the federal government failed to provide a proper basis for demanding the information.

The post A federal judge dismisses another DOJ lawsuit seeking voter data, this time in Massachusetts appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Federal judge dismisses DOJ lawsuit for voter data in Massachusetts on April 9, 2026.
Judge ruled government lacked proper basis for demanding voter information.
Local residents maintain privacy as federal attempts for data collection are blocked.

articles.title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
articles.description:
"We know this place is going to be full of Boston Fleet fans, but it's a step in the right direction for women's hockey in general.”

The post The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston Fleet to play at sold-out TD Garden on April 9, 2026.
Aerin Frankel and Megan Keller highlight the team's commitment to women's hockey.
Event signifies growth and support for women's hockey in Boston.

articles.title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
articles.description:
The woman is the fifth of six defendants to plead guilty for their involvement in the trafficking ring, which they ran in 2023.

The post Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Fall River woman pleads guilty to leading a sex trafficking ring in 2023.
She is the fifth of six defendants to admit guilt in this case.
The case highlights ongoing issues of trafficking affecting Boston communities.

articles.title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
articles.description:
"We simply hope to locate the owner and provide them with closure,” police said.

The post Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police found two dead dogs on South Shore and seek their owners.
Authorities aim to provide closure to the owners of the deceased pets.
This incident raises concerns about pet safety and responsible ownership in Boston.

articles.title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
articles.description:
"I have learned to carry the unfathomable loss of Micah and channel all that love and energy into fueling the NEC Society’s work."

The post She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for necrotizing enterocolitis appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jennifer Canvasser is running the Boston Marathon to honor her son Micah's memory.
She aims to raise awareness for necrotizing enterocolitis through the NEC Society.
Her efforts highlight the need for increased support and research on this condition in Boston.

articles.title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
articles.description:
Four decades after the victim was murdered at age 20, her family and friends shared statements in remembrance of her.

The post Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Braintree man sentenced to life for the 1983 murder of Salem State student, 20-year-old.
Victim's family and friends shared memories during the sentencing, highlighting her lasting impact.
The case closure brings some justice to the Salem State community after 40 years.
